story.soThat: test pattern'ini öğrenebilleyim ve tutarlı, kaliteli unit testler yazabileyim

criterion.title: UsersService Unit Test Dosyası Oluştur
criterion.given: users modülü için unit test yokken
criterion.then: - Dosya __tests__/ klasörü altında bulunmalı - Arrange-Act-Assert (AAA) pattern kullanılmalı - TestingModule.createTestingModule() ile test setup yapılmalı - UsersRepository ve I18nService mock'lanmalı

criterion.title: Create User Test Cases
criterion.then: - should create user successfully (normal akış) - should throw ConflictException when email exists (duplicate kontrolü) - should hash password before creating user (password hashing kontrolü) - Repository.create() mock'unun doğru parametrelerle çağrıldığı verify edilmeli - Return edilen user object'i beklenen yapıda olmalı

criterion.title: Find User Test Cases
criterion.then: - should return user when found (user bulunduğunda return eder) - should throw NotFoundException when user not found (user bulunamazsa exception) - should throw NotFoundException for soft-deleted user (soft-delete edilmiş user için exception) - Repository.findOne() mock'unun doğru parametrelerle çağrıldığı verify edilmeli - I18nService.t() mock'u translate key ile çağrılmalı

criterion.title: Update User Test Cases
criterion.then: - should update user successfully (normal akış) - should throw NotFoundException when user not found for update - should throw ConflictException when updating to existing email - should not update password if not provided (password optional) - Repository.update() mock'unun doğru parametrelerle çağrıldığı verify edilmeli

criterion.title: Delete User Test Cases
criterion.then: - should soft-delete user successfully (deletedAt set edilmeli) - should throw NotFoundException when user not found for delete - should not hard-delete user (hard delete değil, soft delete yapılmalı) - Repository.update() mock'u deletedAt field'ı ile çağrılmalı

criterion.title: Mock Setup ve Test Isolation
criterion.then: - beforeEach block'unda TestingModule setup yapılmalı - afterEach block'unda jest.clearAllMocks() çağrılmalı - Her test kendi mock data'sını oluşturmalı (shared state yok) - Mock'ların return value'ları her test için spesifik olmalı - Test execution order önemli olmamalı (test isolation)

criterion.title: Coverage ve Performance
criterion.then: - UsersService coverage %80 veya üzeri olmalı - Tüm service layer testleri < 1 saniyede tamamlanmalı - Coverage report'ta uncovered lines açıkça görünmeli - Branch coverage (if/else, error handling) %80+ olmalı

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Testing Infrastructure
doc.section: APIs and Interfaces - TestingModule API Pattern
doc.snippet: Unit Test Setup using TestingModule: Test.createTestingModule() ile providers mock'lama, Arrange-Act-Assert pattern, mock isolation ve cleanup best practices

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Testing Infrastructure
doc.section: Workflows and Sequencing - Unit Test Workflow
doc.snippet: AAA Pattern: 1) ARRANGE (setup test data and mocks), 2) ACT (execute method under test), 3) ASSERT (verify results), 4) CLEANUP (jest.clearAllMocks)

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Testing Infrastructure
doc.section: Data Models and Contracts - Test Configuration Schema
doc.snippet: Coverage Thresholds: Global 70%, Services 80%, Common 100%. jest.config.js konfigürasyonu ile coverage enforcement

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Testing Infrastructure
doc.section: Test Strategy Summary - Unit Testing Strategy
doc.snippet: Focus: Service layer, utility functions, guards. Approach: Mock all dependencies, fast execution. Tools: Jest, @nestjs/testing, TestingModule. Pattern: AAA

doc.title: Epic 9: Testing Infrastructure
doc.snippet: As a developer, I want service layer unit test examples, so that test yazabileyim. Acceptance Criteria: UsersService tests, AAA pattern, mocking, coverage 80%+, fast execution

doc.title: Story 9.1: Jest Configuration & Test Setup
doc.snippet: Test utilities in test/utils/ (mockI18nService, mockLogger, mockPrismaService) created in Epic 8.5-4. Coverage thresholds active (Global 70%, Services 80%, Common 100%). Path aliases (@/) working in tests

artifact.reason: Service under test. Contains methods: findOne, updateProfile, findAll, create, update, remove. Uses PrismaService and I18nService dependencies that need mocking
Existing test file showing AAA pattern, TestingModule setup, PrismaService and I18nService mocking. Good reference for test structure and mock patterns. Contains findOne, updateProfile test examples
Reusable I18nService mock factory from Epic 8.5-4. Returns jest.fn() mocks for t() and translate() methods. Can be imported and used in UsersService tests
Reusable PrismaService mock with all model methods (findFirst, findMany, create, update, etc). Created in Epic 8.5-4. Alternative to inline mock creation
Central export for all test utilities. Allows clean imports: import { createMockI18nService } from 'test/utils'
DTO for user creation. Used as input type for create tests. Contains email, password, firstName, lastName, phoneNumber fields
DTO for user updates. Used as input type for update tests. Contains partial user fields
Jest configuration from Story 9.1. Coverage thresholds: Services 80%, Common 100%, Global 70%. Path aliases, coverage reporters configured

package.name: jest
package.usage: Core test framework for test runner, assertions, mocking

package.name: @nestjs/testing
package.usage: TestingModule for dependency injection in tests, test app bootstrap

package.name: ts-jest
package.usage: TypeScript preprocessor to compile TS test files

package.name: @types/jest
package.usage: Jest type definitions for TypeScript support

package.name: @nestjs/common
package.usage: Exception classes (NotFoundException, ConflictException) for assertion testing

package.name: nestjs-i18n
package.usage: I18nService for internationalization - needs mocking in tests

package.name: @prisma/client
package.usage: Prisma Client types (User entity), PrismaService needs mocking

package.name: bcrypt
package.usage: Password hashing - mock bcrypt.hash in tests

constraints.constraint: Arrange-Act-Assert (AAA) pattern mandatory: 1. ARRANGE: Setup mock data, configure mock return values, prepare test input 2. ACT: Call the method under test with test input 3. ASSERT: Verify return value, check mock method calls, validate exceptions 4. CLEANUP: jest.clearAllMocks() in afterEach [Source: docs/tech-spec-epic-9.md#Workflows-and-Sequencing]

Mock all dependencies using TestingModule: - PrismaService: Mock with jest.fn() for user.findFirst, user.create, user.update - I18nService: Mock with jest.fn() for translate method (or use test/utils/mockI18nService.ts) - Logger: Suppress logs or mock Logger class - NO real database connections in unit tests [Source: docs/tech-spec-epic-9.md#Test-Strategy-Summary]

Service layer coverage must be >= 80%: - Branch coverage 80%+ - Function coverage 80%+ - Line coverage 80%+ - Statement coverage 80%+ Enforced by jest.config.js coverageThreshold for './src/**/services/**/*.ts' [Source: jest.config.js lines 29-34, docs/tech-spec-epic-9.md]

Each test must be independent: - No shared state between tests - Mock reset in afterEach with jest.clearAllMocks() - Each test creates its own mock data - Test execution order must not matter [Source: docs/tech-spec-epic-9.md#Non-Functional-Requirements → Reliability]

Unit tests must be fast: - Service test execution < 1 second for entire suite - No real database queries (use mocks) - No async delays or timeouts - Immediate mock responses [Source: docs/tech-spec-epic-9.md#Non-Functional-Requirements → Performance]

Test file naming convention: - Unit tests: *.spec.ts (e.g., users.service.spec.ts) - Integration tests: *.integration-spec.ts (Story 9.3, not this story) - E2E tests: *.e2e-spec.ts (Story 9.4, not this story) - Location: src/modules/users/__tests__/ or next to source file [Source: docs/tech-spec-epic-9.md#System-Architecture-Alignment]

Test names must be descriptive: - Format: "should [expected behavior] when [condition]" - Example: "should throw NotFoundException when user not found" - NOT: "test1", "testCreate", etc. - Helps debugging and documentation [Source: docs/tech-spec-epic-9.md#Test-Strategy-Summary]

interface.name: UsersService.findOne
interface.signature: async findOne(id: string, domainID: string): Promise<User>
interface.description: Finds user by ID with multi-tenancy and soft-delete filtering. Throws NotFoundException if user not found or soft-deleted. Uses prisma.user.findFirst() and i18n.translate()

interface.name: UsersService.updateProfile
interface.signature: async updateProfile(userId: string, domainID: string, updateProfileDto: UpdateProfileDto): Promise<User>
interface.description: Updates user profile with validation and uniqueness checks. Verifies user exists, checks phoneNumber uniqueness, updates user. Throws NotFoundException or ConflictException

interface.name: UsersService.findAll
interface.signature: async findAll(queryDto: QueryUserDto, domainID: string): Promise<{data: User[]; total: number}>
interface.description: Finds all users with pagination, filtering, and sorting. Admin operation. Returns object with data array and total count

interface.name: TestingModule.createTestingModule
interface.description: Creates testing module with mock providers. Pattern: provide service under test + mock dependencies with useValue. Call .compile() to get TestingModule, then module.get<Service>(Service) to get instance

interface.name: createMockI18nService
interface.description: Factory function for I18nService mock. Returns object with jest.fn() mocks for t(), translate(), lang() methods. Reusable across all tests

interface.name: PrismaService.user
interface.signature: user: { findFirst, findMany, create, update, count, ... }
interface.description: Prisma user model delegate. Methods to mock in tests: findFirst (find one user), create (create user), update (update user), findMany (find multiple users), count (count users)

tests.standards: Testing standards for Story 9.2: - Framework: Jest v30.x with @nestjs/testing - Pattern: Arrange-Act-Assert (AAA) in every test - Mock Strategy: TestingModule with useValue for dependency injection - Test Isolation: beforeEach setup, afterEach jest.clearAllMocks() - Coverage Target: 80%+ for service layer (branches, functions, lines, statements) - Performance: Unit tests < 1 second execution - File Location: src/modules/users/__tests__/users.service.spec.ts OR src/modules/users/services/users.service.spec.ts - Naming Convention: describe() for service/method groups, it() with "should [action] when [condition]" - Reuse test utilities from test/utils/ (mockI18nService, mockPrismaService) [Source: docs/tech-spec-epic-9.md, jest.config.js, docs/stories/9-1-jest-configuration-test-setup.md]

ideas.idea: Setup test structure: - Import Test, TestingModule from @nestjs/testing - Import UsersService, PrismaService, I18nService - describe('UsersService') block - beforeEach: Create TestingModule with mocked PrismaService and I18nService - afterEach: jest.clearAllMocks() - Get service instance: module.get<UsersService>(UsersService)

Create user tests: - Test: "should create user successfully" - Mock prisma.user.findFirst(null), prisma.user.create(user), assert return value - Test: "should throw ConflictException when email exists" - Mock prisma.user.findFirst(existingUser), assert ConflictException thrown - Test: "should hash password before creating user" - Mock bcrypt.hash, verify prisma.user.create called with hashedPassword

Find user tests: - Test: "should return user when found" - Mock prisma.user.findFirst(user), assert return value matches - Test: "should throw NotFoundException when user not found" - Mock prisma.user.findFirst(null), assert NotFoundException thrown, verify i18n.translate called - Test: "should throw NotFoundException for soft-deleted user" - Mock prisma.user.findFirst with deletedAt set, assert NotFoundException

Update user tests: - Test: "should update user successfully" - Mock findOne, prisma.user.update, assert updated user returned - Test: "should throw NotFoundException when user not found for update" - Mock findOne to throw NotFoundException - Test: "should throw ConflictException when updating to existing email" - Mock prisma.user.findFirst with conflicting email user - Test: "should not update password if not provided" - Mock prisma.user.update, verify password field not included

Delete user tests: - Test: "should soft-delete user successfully" - Mock findOne, prisma.user.update with deletedAt, assert deletedAt set - Test: "should throw NotFoundException when user not found for delete" - Mock findOne to throw NotFoundException - Test: "should not hard-delete user" - Verify prisma.user.delete NOT called, only prisma.user.update with deletedAt

Test isolation: - Verify jest.clearAllMocks() called in afterEach - Each test creates its own mock data (no shared mockUser variable mutations) - Mock return values set per test in ARRANGE phase - Run tests in random order to verify independence

Coverage validation: - Run: npm run test:cov - Open: coverage/index.html - Verify: UsersService coverage >= 80% (branches, functions, lines, statements) - Run: npm test users.service.spec - Verify: Execution time < 1 second - Add tests for uncovered branches if needed
